project.name: Notification Email Template Standardization
project.description: Migrate the standalone notification.blade.php email template to use the standardized <x-mail::message> component system with proper Mailable class, role-based unsubscribe URLs, and consistent branding across all notification emails.

user_stories.title: Create UnsubscribeUrlResolver service
user_stories.description: Extract unsubscribe URL resolution logic into a dedicated service that returns the correct URL based on recipient role. Tenants get a 30-day temporary signed route to email.preferences. Landlords get the authenticated notifications.settings route. Unknown roles get null (omits link from footer).
steps: Write failing test: tenant user returns a signed URL containing 'email/preferences' with 'signature' query param
Write failing test: landlord user returns the notifications.settings route URL
Write failing test: caretaker user returns the notifications.settings route URL
Write failing test: user with unknown role returns null
Create app/Services/Notification/UnsubscribeUrlResolver.php with resolve(User $recipient): ?string method
For tenants: return URL::temporarySignedRoute('email.preferences', now()->addDays(30), ['user' => $recipient->id])
For landlords/caretakers: return route('notifications.settings')
For other roles: return null
Run tests green, run Pint
acceptance_criteria: Tenant user receives a valid signed URL pointing to email.preferences route
Landlord user receives the notifications.settings route URL (not signed)
Caretaker user receives the notifications.settings route URL
Unknown or missing role returns null
Signed URL expires in 30 days

user_stories.title: Create NotificationMail Mailable class
user_stories.description: Create a proper Mailable class that wraps notification data (subject, message, data array, recipient) and uses a markdown template. Does NOT implement ShouldQueue since queuing is handled at the job level (SendNotificationJob, SendBulkNotificationsJob). Uses UnsubscribeUrlResolver for the unsubscribe URL.
steps: Write failing test: NotificationMail renders with correct subject in envelope
Write failing test: NotificationMail passes message, data, and recipient to template
Write failing test: NotificationMail resolves unsubscribe URL via UnsubscribeUrlResolver for tenant
Write failing test: NotificationMail resolves unsubscribe URL via UnsubscribeUrlResolver for landlord
Create app/Mail/NotificationMail.php extending Mailable (NOT ShouldQueue)
Constructor accepts: string $notificationSubject, string $notificationMessage, ?array $data, User $recipient
Envelope returns $notificationSubject
Content uses markdown: 'emails.notification' with unsubscribeUrl from UnsubscribeUrlResolver
Inject UnsubscribeUrlResolver in content() via app() helper
Run tests green, run Pint
acceptance_criteria: Mailable renders without errors
Subject line matches notification subject
Template receives all required variables (subject, message, data, recipient, unsubscribeUrl)
Does NOT implement ShouldQueue
Tenant recipient gets signed unsubscribe URL in rendered HTML
Landlord recipient gets notifications.settings URL in rendered HTML
definition_of_done: Mailable can be instantiated and rendered without errors

user_stories.title: Migrate notification.blade.php to x-mail::message markdown
user_stories.description: Rewrite the standalone HTML notification template to use the standardized <x-mail::message> component system. Preserve all dynamic features: greeting, message body, data key-value table, action button. Replace hardcoded 'PropManager Team' with config('app.name'). The data table renders arbitrary key-value pairs from the $data array, filtering out non-scalar values and internal keys (action_url, action_text). The action button uses x-mail::button when action_url is present.
steps: Write failing test: rendered HTML contains x-mail layout structure (check for vendor layout CSS classes)
Write failing test: rendered HTML contains recipient greeting with name
Write failing test: rendered HTML contains escaped message body with line breaks preserved
Write failing test: rendered HTML contains data table when $data has scalar key-value pairs
Write failing test: rendered HTML does NOT render array/object values in data table
Write failing test: rendered HTML contains action button when data has action_url and action_text
Write failing test: rendered HTML omits action button when data lacks action_url
Write failing test: footer shows config('app.name') Team, NOT hardcoded 'PropManager Team'
Write failing test: rendered HTML contains unsubscribe link when unsubscribeUrl is provided
Write failing test: HTML entities in $message are escaped (XSS prevention)
Rewrite resources/views/emails/notification.blade.php using <x-mail::message :unsubscribeUrl="$unsubscribeUrl ?? null">
Use markdown heading for subject, paragraph for greeting
Wrap message body in <x-mail::panel> with {!! nl2br(e($message)) !!}
Render data table: filter $data to exclude action_url, action_text, and non-scalar values; render as HTML table within markdown
Add <x-mail::button :url="$data['action_url']"> when action_url present
Footer: {{ config('app.name') }} Team
Run all tests green, verify visual output via assertSeeInHtml/assertDontSeeInHtml
Run Pint
acceptance_criteria: Template uses <x-mail::message> wrapper with unsubscribeUrl prop
Greeting shows recipient name
Message body is escaped (e()) with newlines converted to
Data table renders only scalar key-value pairs from $data
Internal keys (action_url, action_text) excluded from data table
Action button renders as <x-mail::button> when action_url present
Footer uses config('app.name') not hardcoded string
Unsubscribe link appears in footer when URL provided
No standalone <style> or <html> tags remain (uses vendor layout)
HTML injection in $message is neutralized by e()
definition_of_done: Visual consistency with other email templates (uses same CSS theme)

user_stories.title: Update NotificationService::sendEmail() to use NotificationMail
user_stories.description: Replace the closure-based Mail::send() in NotificationService::sendEmail() (line 414-435) with the new NotificationMail Mailable. Keep synchronous sending (Mail::to()->send(), NOT queue()). Preserve the try/catch with markAsSent/markAsFailed. The Mailable handles template rendering and unsubscribe URL resolution internally.
steps: Write failing test: sendEmail() dispatches a NotificationMail instance (use Mail::fake())
Write failing test: NotificationMail receives correct subject, message, data, and recipient
Write failing test: notification is marked as sent after successful email dispatch
Write failing test: notification is marked as failed when Mail throws exception
Update sendEmail() to: Mail::to($recipient->email)->send(new NotificationMail(...))
Pass $notification->subject, $notification->message, $notification->data, $recipient to Mailable constructor
Keep existing try/catch with markAsSent() and markAsFailed()
Run existing NotificationServiceTest suite to verify no regressions
Run Pint
acceptance_criteria: sendEmail() uses NotificationMail Mailable instead of closure-based Mail::send()
Email is sent synchronously (Mail::send, not Mail::queue)
markAsSent() called on success
markAsFailed() called on exception with error message
All existing NotificationServiceTest tests still pass
Notification type (rent_reminder, arrears_notice, etc.) does not affect email rendering — all use same template
user_stories.verification: php artisan test --filter=NotificationServiceTest && php artisan test --filter=NotificationMailTest

user_stories.title: Content safety tests for user-configured notification templates
user_stories.description: Verify that user-configured notification body content (from NotificationTemplate::render()) is safe when rendered in the migrated email template. Test HTML injection, XSS attempts, and edge cases like empty body, very long body, and special characters. The template uses e() for escaping which should handle all cases.
steps: Write test: template body with <script>alert('xss')</script> renders as escaped text, not executable script
Write test: template body with renders as escaped text
Write test: empty $message renders email without error
Write test: $message with only whitespace/newlines renders correctly
Write test: $data array with null/empty values handles gracefully
Write test: $data with nested arrays does not render nested values in table
Write test: placeholder values containing HTML in NotificationTemplate::render() output are escaped by email template
Run all content safety tests green
acceptance_criteria: HTML tags in $message are entity-escaped in rendered output
Script tags never appear unescaped in rendered HTML
Empty/null message does not crash rendering
Data array edge cases handled (null values, nested arrays, empty array)
Placeholder-injected HTML in template render output is escaped
definition_of_done: No XSS vectors found in rendered output

user_stories.title: End-to-end integration tests for notification email delivery
user_stories.description: Feature tests that exercise the full notification email flow: NotificationService::send() -> channel selection -> sendEmail() -> NotificationMail -> template rendering. Test both tenant and landlord recipients. Test bulk notifications to verify per-recipient unsubscribe URLs. Verify backward compatibility with DB-stored NotificationTemplate rendering.
steps: Write feature test: sendRentReminder() to tenant produces email with standardized layout and signed unsubscribe URL
Write feature test: sendArrearsNotice() to tenant produces email with data table showing arrears details
Write feature test: send() to landlord recipient produces email with notifications.settings unsubscribe URL
Write feature test: sendBulk() to multiple tenants generates unique signed URLs per recipient
Write feature test: notification with action_url in data renders clickable button
Write feature test: notification without data array renders email without table section
Write feature test: email uses config('app.name') not hardcoded 'PropManager'
Write feature test: NotificationTemplate::render() output is correctly passed through to email body
Verify all tests run against existing test database with RefreshDatabase
Run full test suite to confirm no regressions
acceptance_criteria: Rent reminder email to tenant uses standardized layout
Arrears notice email to tenant shows data table
Landlord email has different unsubscribe URL than tenant email
Bulk send creates per-recipient signed URLs (not shared)
Action button renders when action_url present in data
App name from config used in footer
DB-stored template body renders correctly in new layout
No existing tests broken
definition_of_done: Full test suite passes (php artisan test)
user_stories.verification: php artisan test --filter=NotificationEmailStandardizationTest && php artisan test

user_stories.title: RFC 8058 List-Unsubscribe headers and one-click unsubscribe endpoint
user_stories.description: Add RFC 8058 compliant List-Unsubscribe and List-Unsubscribe-Post email headers to NotificationMail. Since June 2024, Google and Yahoo require these headers for bulk senders. Add a POST /email/unsubscribe endpoint for machine one-click unsubscribe that disables email_enabled on NotificationPreference. The existing GET /email/preferences body link is unchanged.
steps: Write failing tests for UnsubscribeUrlResolver::resolveForHeader() (RED)
Implement resolveForHeader() returning signed POST URL for tenants (GREEN)
Write failing tests for NotificationMail::headers() with List-Unsubscribe (RED)
Implement headers() method on NotificationMail (GREEN)
Add POST /email/unsubscribe route with signed+throttle middleware
Write failing tests for oneClickUnsubscribe endpoint (RED)
Implement NotificationsController::oneClickUnsubscribe() (GREEN)

Add security log entry in oneClickUnsubscribe() after disabling email (include actor user_id, tenant landlord_id, action 'email_unsubscribe', request IP) and write test assertion verifying the security log is created
Run Pint and phpmd, run full test suite
acceptance_criteria: Tenant emails include List-Unsubscribe header with angle-bracketed signed URL
Tenant emails include List-Unsubscribe-Post: List-Unsubscribe=One-Click header
Super admin/unknown role emails omit List-Unsubscribe headers
POST /email/unsubscribe with valid signed URL returns 200 and disables email
POST /email/unsubscribe without signature returns 403
POST /email/unsubscribe for non-tenant returns 403
Existing GET /email/preferences body link unchanged
Security log records unsubscribe events
definition_of_done: All unit and feature tests pass
phpmd clean on modified files (no new violations)
user_stories.verification: php artisan test --filter=UnsubscribeUrlResolverTest && php artisan test --filter=NotificationMailTest && php artisan test --filter=NotificationEmailStandardizationTest

user_stories.title: Cleanup: remove hardcoded branding, verify no dangling references, E2E
user_stories.description: Fix 12 blade files with hardcoded 'PropManager' branding. Grep for any remaining references to old template patterns. Verify notification history UI renders correctly. Run E2E browser tests for notification flow verification.
steps: Grep codebase for 'emails.notification' to confirm only NotificationMail references it
Fix 12 blade files with hardcoded 'PropManager' to use config('app.name')
Grep for any remaining inline <style> tags in email blade files
Verify notification history UI still renders correctly
Run E2E browser verification for notification pages
Run full test suite
Run Pint on all modified files
acceptance_criteria: No hardcoded 'PropManager' in any blade template
All templates use config('app.name')
Notification history UI unaffected
All tests pass
Pint clean
definition_of_done: Pint passes on all modified files

metadata.critical_path: NOTIF-TPL-001 -> NOTIF-TPL-002 -> NOTIF-TPL-003 -> NOTIF-TPL-004 -> NOTIF-TPL-006 -> NOTIF-TPL-007 -> NOTIF-TPL-008
